title: Cairo 10-Day Heritage Tour
metaTitle: Cairo 10-Day Heritage Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 10-day trip to Cairo, Egypt: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Pyramids of Giza & Great Sphinx and Grand Egyptian Museum. Prices from $1431 per person (estimate).
highlights: Quad biking in the desert near Giza
overview: Spend 10 days discovering Cairo, Egypt — Desert climate with mild winters (highs 19°C) and extremely hot, dry summers (highs 36°C); rainfall is minimal year-round. October to April offers comfortable, mild temperatures for exploring the pyramids and outdoor sites away from the intense summer heat. This itinerary blends Pyramids of Giza & Great Sphinx, Grand Egyptian Museum, Khan el-Khalili bazaar (browsing) with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
9 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Pyramids of Giza & Great Sphinx, Grand Egyptian Museum, Khan el-Khalili bazaar (browsing)
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Cairo
itinerary.description: Land at Cairo International Airport (CAI) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Pyramids of Giza & Great Sphinx — Last surviving Ancient Wonder of the World. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Cairo: Grand Egyptian Museum & Egyptian Museum (Tahrir Square)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Grand Egyptian Museum — Massive new museum showcasing Tutankhamun's treasures. In the afternoon, continue to Egyptian Museum (Tahrir Square) — Historic museum with an extensive antiquities collection. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Cairo: Saqqara Step Pyramid & Citadel of Saladin & Muhammad Ali Mosque
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Saqqara Step Pyramid — World's oldest monumental stone structure. In the afternoon, continue to Citadel of Saladin & Muhammad Ali Mosque — Medieval fortress with sweeping city views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Citadel of Saladin & Muhammad Ali Mosque

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Saqqara and Dahshur pyramids
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Saqqara and Dahshur pyramids, a popular side trip from Cairo. Return to Cairo in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Saqqara and Dahshur pyramids

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Manial Palace Museum — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Cairo: Khan el-Khalili guided tour & Nile dinner cruise with show
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Khan el-Khalili guided tour — Historic bazaar (browsing free, tours optional). In the afternoon, continue to Nile dinner cruise with show — Evening cruise with belly dance and Tanoura performance. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Alexandria
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Alexandria, a popular side trip from Cairo. Return to Cairo in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Cairo: Great Pyramid interior chamber entry & Memphis open-air museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Great Pyramid interior chamber entry — Additional ticket to enter inside the Great Pyramid. In the afternoon, continue to Memphis open-air museum — Ancient capital site with a colossal Ramesses II statue. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Al-Muizz Street after dark — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Sound and Light Show at Giza or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to CAI for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Grand Egyptian Museum and pyramid interior tickets online in advance to manage queue times.
Hire a licensed Egyptologist guide at Giza — context transforms the site and helps navigate persistent vendors.
Agree on ride fares upfront or use Uber/Careem instead of street-hailed taxis.
Visit the pyramids at opening time (8am) to avoid both the heat and the largest tour groups.
Carry small EGP bills for tipping (baksheesh), which is expected for many services.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest clothing covering shoulders and knees, especially for women
Comfortable closed shoes for dusty archaeological sites
Sun protection — hat, sunglasses, high-SPF sunscreen
A light scarf for mosque visits and sun coverage
thingsToDo: Citadel of Saladin & Muhammad Ali Mosque
Sound and Light Show at Giza
Quad biking in the desert near Giza
Hot air balloon over Luxor (extension trip)
thingsToAvoid: Camel/horse touts at Giza who quote one price and demand much more upon dismount — agree firmly in writing or with a guide present.
Unofficial "museum closed, follow me" street scams designed to redirect you to a shop.
Drinking tap water — stick to sealed bottled water throughout the trip.
Walking alone late at night in unfamiliar districts outside central tourist areas.
Buying "ancient artifacts" from street sellers — nearly always fake, and export of real antiquities is illegal.
topRestaurants: The Nile Ritz-Carlton restaurants — International Fine Dining ($$$$)
Naguib Mahfouz Café — Egyptian, Khan el-Khalili ($$)
familyActivities: Camel or horse ride at Giza Plateau
adventureActivities: Quad biking in the desert near Giza
Hot air balloon over Luxor (extension trip)
luxuryExperiences: Nile-view suite at a 5-star riverside hotel
Private felucca sunset sail with catering
VIP early access to the Grand Egyptian Museum
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset felucca sail on the Nile
Private candlelit dinner overlooking the pyramids
Nile dinner cruise with live entertainment
Romantic hot air balloon extension in Luxor
budgetExperiences: Free views of the pyramids from public viewpoints
Wander Khan el-Khalili bazaar and sip tea at El Fishawy
Al-Azhar Park for a low-cost afternoon with skyline views
Koshari street food for an authentic cheap meal
localEtiquette: Dress modestly, particularly for women — shoulders and knees covered is respectful outside resort areas.
Use your right hand for eating and handing over items; the left is considered unclean.
Bargaining is standard and expected in markets like Khan el-Khalili.
Ask permission before photographing people, especially women.
During Ramadan, avoid eating, drinking or smoking in public during daylight hours.
localTransportGuide: Cairo's traffic is notoriously heavy, so the metro is often the fastest way across the city center, with a dedicated women-only car available. Uber and Careem ride-hailing apps are widely used and generally more reliable and transparent on price than hailing a taxi on the street.
bestTimeToVisit: October to April offers comfortable, mild temperatures for exploring the pyramids and outdoor sites away from the intense summer heat.
weather: Desert climate with mild winters (highs 19°C) and extremely hot, dry summers (highs 36°C); rainfall is minimal year-round.
visaInformation: Most Western nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens can obtain a visa-on-arrival or e-Visa for tourist stays; e-Visa application in advance online is recommended to avoid airport queues. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~9.8h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Cairo?
faqs.answer: Most Western nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens can obtain a visa-on-arrival or e-Visa for tourist stays; e-Visa application in advance online is recommended to avoid airport queues. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Cairo?
faqs.answer: October to April offers comfortable, mild temperatures for exploring the pyramids and outdoor sites away from the intense summer heat. Desert climate with mild winters (highs 19°C) and extremely hot, dry summers (highs 36°C); rainfall is minimal year-round.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Cairo safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Cairo is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Cairo?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EGP. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Sarah Williams
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Cairo is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Khan el-Khalili guided tour. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Sarah Silva
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Cairo is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Zamalek neighborhood. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Ethan Costa
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Al-Muizz Street and Memphis open-air museum. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Nguyen
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Al-Muizz Street alone was worth the trip, but combined with Giza Plateau viewpoint (outside ticketed area) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Ava Kim
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Memphis open-air museum alone was worth the trip, but combined with Giza Plateau viewpoint (outside ticketed area) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Omar Andersen
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Egyptian Museum (Tahrir Square) and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Chen Ivanov
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Grand Egyptian Museum in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.
